subject: Statistics
title: The Prosecutor's Fallacy
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Prosecutor's Fallacy.

hook.text: DNA matches the suspect with 1-in-a-million accuracy. Guilty? Not so fast — in a city of 8 million, expect 8 matches.

buildup.text: The fallacy confuses the probability of evidence given innocence with the probability of innocence given evidence.

discovery.text: P(match | innocent) = 0.000001. But P(innocent | match) depends on how many people were tested.

twist.text: Sally Clark was wrongly convicted partly due to this fallacy. A doctor misstated the probability in court.

climax.text: Bayes' theorem is the correct framework. The prior probability of guilt matters as much as the evidence.

punchline.text: Rare evidence doesn't mean rare innocence. The fallacy jails people.

quiz.question: What does the prosecutor's fallacy confuse?
options: The probability of evidence given innocence with the probability of innocence given evidence
The guilt of the suspect with the length of the sentence
